title: If Grief For Grief Can Touch Thee
content:
If grief for grief can touch thee,
    If answering woe for woe,
    If any truth can melt thee
    Come to me now!

    I cannot be more lonely,
    More drear I cannot be!
    My worn heart beats so wildly
    'Twill break for thee.

    And when the world despises,
    When Heaven repels my prayer,
    Will not mine angel comfort?
    Mine idol hear?

    Yes, by the tears I'm poured,
    By all my hours of pain
    O I shall surely win thee,
    Beloved, again!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Bronte, titled "If Grief For Grief Can Touch Thee", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
